clusters.justification: These terms co-activate within cortical-subcortical affective circuits, primarily involving the amygdala and anterior insula, which mediate the internal generation, processing, and regulation of emotional states.

Bound by the mesolimbic dopamine system, ventral striatum, and orbitofrontal cortex, these terms reflect the macroscopic system dedicated to computing subjective reward, reinforcing motivation, and guiding goal-directed choice.

Grounded in the functional topography of the Default Mode Network, these terms represent a unified axis of internally directed processing used to construct abstract mental models of the self, others, and social narratives.
